message:
feat(core): implement ANALYZE/REINDEX with sqlite_stat1 support and flatten simple FROM-subqueries

Replace the ANALYZE/REINDEX no-op stubs with full implementations that
match SQLite behavior. ANALYZE populates sqlite_stat1 with per-index
row-count and prefix-distinctness statistics; REINDEX clears and
repopulates index B-trees. Both are now marked as write statements.
Extract backfill_index() from CREATE INDEX for reuse by REINDEX.

Add a query rewrite pass that eliminates trivial derived tables
(SELECT ... FROM (SELECT ... FROM table)) by merging inner and outer
queries into a single direct table scan, moving the dispatch decision
after the rewrite phase so flattened queries use the VDBE table-backed
path. Conformance oracle test verifies stat1 output matches rusqlite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
